issues: flame silhouette too rigid/straight — lacks the dynamic upward taper and dancing quality expected for charming Hyrule warmth
flame highlight zone could push further toward saturated lemon-yellow at the tip for stronger brightness read
overall silhouette is elongated and functional rather than round/friendly toy-diorama proportion
reasoning: Emotional (60%): The palette is warm and appropriately saturated — orange body, amber mid-tones, warm brown handle, with small accent details at the base wrap. The torch reads as friendly and non-threatening, fitting the Hyrule mood. However, the flame shape is somewhat stiff and columnar rather than having the buoyant, upward-tapering silhouette that gives classic Zelda torches their charming, lively quality. The toy-diorama compactness is not strongly present — the sprite feels tall and utilitarian. Emotional score: ~6/10. Technical (40%): Hard 1px outlines are clearly present and pixel-grid-snapped at all zoom levels. Shading uses 2-3 distinct hard-edged tones (dark orange shadow, mid orange body, yellow-orange highlight) with no anti-aliasing bleed visible. Pixel consistency and hand-drawn quality hold well at 2× and 4×. Denzi reference comparison shows similar outline weight and tonal discipline; the torch matches that category standard. Technical score: ~7/10. Weighted: 0.6×6 + 0.4×7 = 6.4 → 6. Marginal — passes technical gate but needs flame shape expressiveness and color brightness boost to reach pass tier.
